title: Phase 1 Any-Cleanup Plan Review — 7-Axis Judgment

coupling.note: No new cross-module dependencies except RuntimeEvent import (validation-parser-worker) — already peer-validated against existing ../../event import at L5. Exit added to existing effect import in typed-result.ts. Zero new package deps.

debuggability.note: Catch clause changes improve debuggability by adding instanceof Error guards — non-Error throws now log String(e) instead of undefined. Claims.ts structural types produce opaque compile-time errors rather than runtime — acceptable because they fail at build. Missing: Effect.withSpan annotations on new catch paths.

convergence.note: Moves TOWARD typed codebase. But claims.ts introduces local ClaimRow type instead of using Drizzle $inferSelect — creates parallel type definition drift risk. Validation-parser-worker RuntimeEvent coupling ties worker to event schema evolution, though this is the correct dependency.

surface_area.note: No public API changes. All affected functions are internal. buildPathTree return type changes internally but caller destructures result flexibly (as array of anonymous objects). Zero export signature changes.

testability.note: MAJOR CONCERN: Plan verification is typecheck-only (bun typecheck). Claims.ts structural types cannot be verified by typecheck alone because Object.entries on Record<string, unknown> yields [string, unknown], making value.file a type error requiring casts. Catch clause guards change runtime behavior — no runtime test coverage planned. Validation-parser-worker RuntimeEvent access patterns need runtime verification because payloadJson is Schema.Unknown.

error_clarity.note: Catch clause changes improve error logging (non-Error throws now show String(e)). Claims.ts type errors will be at compile time — good. Validation-parser-worker preserves existing fallback-to-unknown pattern which silently masks malformed events. No span annotations added.

reversibility.note: Each change is per-file and independently reversible. Plan lists full git checkout command for rollback. Exception: claims.ts L128 type change is interdependent with L177-178 — reverting L128 requires reverting L177-178 too. Catch clause guards are additive — reverting returns to original behavior.

objections.evidence: Plan verification is typecheck-only. The claims.ts L128 change introduces Record<string, { file?: PathTreeNode; dir?: { children: Record<string, unknown> ... } }>. Object.entries on Record<string, unknown> yields [string, unknown], making value.file a type error. The plan acknowledges this (medium risk: claims_tree_intermediate_type) but provides no test strategy beyond bun typecheck.

ClaimRow type does NOT exist in codebase. Grep across all packages/opencode — zero matches. Plan audit trail references claimRowToType in coordination.ts but that returns TaskClaim, not ClaimRow. The plan assumes a type that must be created from scratch.

validation-parser-worker.ts L11: RuntimeEvent.payloadJson is Schema.optional(Schema.Unknown) = unknown | undefined. The existing pattern event.payloadJson as Record<string, unknown> is a brute-force cast from unknown — this defeats the purpose of removing any. The type annotation changes from any to RuntimeEvent, but the payload access still uses an unchecked as cast that could be typeof p === 'object' && p !== null instead.

conditions_for_approval.condition: Explicitly add 'import type { RuntimeEvent } from "../../event/runtime-event"' to validation-parser-worker.ts. The plan references RuntimeEvent but omits the import line. Witness confirmed: RuntimeEvent type is at ../../event/runtime-event (not the barrel export, which is a namespace).

In claims.ts, use the already-inferred type from the Map constructor: (typeof claimRows[0]) | null (line 95-99 already uses this). This is the Drizzle-inferred row type available in scope. Do NOT use phantom ClaimRow.

Add a runtime test for the claims.ts /tree endpoint to verify the new PathTreeNode structural types produce correct JSON. Minimum: 'cd packages/opencode && bun test --test-name-pattern claims'. Typecheck alone cannot verify structural correctness of Object.entries on Record<string, unknown>.

In claims.ts L128, use a properly recursive DirNode type for the dir.children field instead of Record<string, unknown>. The current plan's Record<string, unknown> for children loses type information through the recursive convert() function. Use: 'type DirNode = { file?: PathTreeNode; dir?: { children: Record<string, DirNode>; claim?: ClaimRow | null; status?: string } }'.

In validation-parser-worker.ts, replace 'event.payloadJson as Record<string, unknown>' with a runtime type guard: 'typeof p === "object" && p !== null ? (p as Record<string, unknown>) : undefined'. This makes the type safety real rather than illusory and preserves the existing fallback behavior.

follow_up_issues.summary: claims.ts creates a local ClaimRow type mirroring Drizzle schema. After Phase 1, file a migration finding to switch to $inferSelect. Document in docs/findings/out-of-scope-findings.jsonl.
extractTestName/extractFilePath/extractLine return fallback string 'unknown' — this silently masks malformed events. Consider structured logging when fallback is hit for observability.
Phase 2 should address: imgMsg(msg: any) in copilot.ts (deferred), the 42 MEDIUM-category items from the inventory, and the EventBridge/MachineState generics enabling safer as-any removal.

notable_omissions_from_plan: The plan does not specify the import line for RuntimeEvent in validation-parser-worker.ts — it references the type but leaves the import implicit.
The plan does not specify where Exit is added to the import in typed-result.ts — it acknowledges the gap but the execution instructions are not explicit.

The plan claims 'no runtime logic changes' in its scope section but the 3 catch clause changes add runtime instanceof Error guards — these ARE runtime logic changes, albeit beneficial ones that fix latent bugs.
best_alternative_considered: Split Phase 1 into two sub-phases: 1a (trivial removals + catch guards — 23 changes across 13 files, zero risk) applied and pushed immediately, and 1b (claims.ts + validation-parser-worker — 9 changes across 2 files) held for structural type design review. This would de-risk the highest-impact changes without blocking the 23 trivially safe improvements.

subagent_results.witness: ses_18249a9c1ffeSro6h6ES0GFuOo — DONE: confirmed 2 missing imports, all other dependencies valid
subagent_results.coroner: DONE — 3/4 scenarios improved, 1 neutral, 1 edge-case regression (extremely unlikely)
subagent_results.blast_radius: DONE — zero consumer impact, all internal-only
subagent_results.reasonable_doubt: DONE — 2 blockers (phantom ClaimRow, claims L128 structural incompatibility), 1 smell (as on unknown backdoor)
subagent_results.exhibit_a: DONE — 4 changes already applied, 2 latent bugs surfaced, 4 missing spans, 1 error-channel gap
subagent_results.appeal: DONE — all groups independent, 2 phantom files (pre-cleaned)

appeal_findings.key_finding: ALL groups independent and individually revertible. Zero cross-group type dependencies.
corrections: smart-bash.ts has zero :any annotations in current working tree — likely pre-cleaned by another lane. Plan should skip.
read-lib.ts has zero :any annotations in current working tree — same.

eventStore.query() returns Effect<RuntimeEvent[], DatabaseError> per event-store.ts:29. After orElseSucceed(()=>[])), events is RuntimeEvent[] — so validation-parser-worker L90 map callback type-safe with RuntimeEvent.
appeal_findings.reversibility: Every batch independently revertible. Flat dependency graph verified.

smells: CLAIMS_TS_BLOCKER: ClaimRow does not exist — zero grep matches in codebase. Must use (typeof claimRows[0]) | null which is already used at L95-99
CLAIMS_TS_L128: Structural type incompatible with mutation pattern. Plan admits fallback to Record<string, unknown> which provides ZERO safety improvement — cosmetic rename only
VALIDATION_PARSER: payloadJson as Record<string, unknown> is backdoor — as on unknown recreates the any safety gap
safe_changes: smart-git.ts L152-154: PASS — TS infers callback types from array
mcp/index.ts L217: PASS — instanceof Error guard is canonical pattern, fixes latent bug
Most remove_annotation changes: PASS — TS inference handles them

already_applied: mcp/index.ts L217 (catch guard) — already in working tree
session/llm.ts L143 (catch guard) — already in working tree
provider/provider.ts L1633 (URL|RequestInfo) — already in working tree
typed-result.ts L505 (Exit.Exit) — already in working tree
latent_bugs_surfaced: claims.ts L203: .name.localeCompare() on undefined — plan type change would expose this as compile error. Need defensive: a.name?.localeCompare(b.name ?? "") ?? 0
validation-parser-worker.ts: extract functions return "unknown" with zero logging — silent data corruption
exhibit_a_findings.error_channel_gap: mcp/index.ts fetchFromClient returns raw e instead of normalizing to Error like sibling listTools does

blockers: claims.ts L128 structural type incompatible with mutation
final_review_status.dirty_files: mcp/index.ts, session/llm.ts, provider/provider.ts, typed-result.ts already have plan changes applied
final_review_status.recommended_action: Split into Phase 1a (23 safe changes, push now) and Phase 1b (claims.ts + validation-parser-worker, redesign required)

blast_radius_findings.verdict: ZERO consumer impact — every change is internal-only. No exported API signatures change. No cross-package impact.
blast_radius_findings.phantom_file: smart-bash.ts has zero :any annotations — plan actually touches coordination.ts instead

final_verdict_summary.safe_to_push_now: 23 changes across 15 files (Groups 1-3: remove_annotation + catch guards + local type replacements)
final_verdict_summary.requires_redesign: 9 changes across 2 files (validation-parser-worker.ts: missing import, RuntimeEvent backdoor; claims.ts: phantom ClaimRow, incompatible structural type, missing defensive sort guard)

improved: mcp catch clauses (scenario 3a/3b): undefined → clear string in logs
coordination.ts (scenario 4): silent runtime → build-time type error
neutral: validation-parser-worker (scenario 2): pre-existing payloadJson as-cast hazard, unchanged
minor_risk: claims.ts (scenario 1): typecheck-only failure with documented fallback, 5-40 min debug
coroner_findings.regression_edge_case: throw { message: "custom" } (plain object) would now log "[object Object]" instead of "custom" — extremely unlikely in Effect codebase which uses Error subclasses
